heading: Administration and operation of noninsured crop assistance program

L. 115–334, § 1601(1)(B)“(i) for which catastrophic risk protection under section 508(b) of the Federal Crop Insurance Act () is not available;7 U.S.C. 1508(b)“(ii) for which additional coverage under subsections (c) and (h) of section 508 of that Act () is not available; and7 U.S.C. 1508“(iii) that is produced for food or fiber.”Subsec. i

children.heading: Administration
children.label: B Administration
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this section through the Farm Service Agency (referred to in this section as the “Agency”).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in this section, the term “eligible crop” means each commercial crop or other agricultural commodity that is produced for food or fiber (except livestock) for which catastrophic risk protection under subsection (b) of section 508 of the Federal Crop Insurance Act () and additional coverage under subsections (c) and (h) of such section are not available or, if such coverage is available, it is only available under a policy that provides coverage for specific intervals based on weather indexes or under a whole farm plan of insurance.7 U.S.C. 1508

children.heading: Crops specifically included
children.label: B Crops specifically included
children.text: The term “eligible crop” shall include floricultural, ornamental nursery, and Christmas tree crops, turfgrass sod, seed crops, aquaculture (including ornamental fish), sea grass and sea oats, camelina, sweet sorghum, biomass sorghum, and industrial crops (including those grown expressly for the purpose of producing a feedstock for renewable biofuel, renewable electricity, or biobased products).

children.heading: Combination of similar types or varieties
children.label: C Combination of similar types or varieties
children.text: At the option of the Secretary, all types or varieties of a crop or commodity, described in subparagraphs (A) and (B), may be considered to be a single eligible crop under this section.

children.heading: Cause of loss
children.label: 3 Cause of loss
children.text: To qualify for assistance under this section, the losses of the noninsured commodity shall be due to drought, flood, or other natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Program reduction in benefits relating to crop production on native sod
children.label: 4 Program reduction in benefits relating to crop production on native sod
i
children.text: on which the plant cover is composed principally of native grasses, grasslike plants, forbs, or shrubs suitable for grazing and browsing; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that has never been tilled, or the producer cannot substantiate that the ground has ever been tilled, for the production of an annual crop as of the date of enactment of this paragraph.

children.heading: Agricultural Act of 2014
children.label: I Agricultural Act of 2014
children.text: During the first 4 crop years of planting, as determined by the Secretary, native sod acreage that has been tilled for the production of an annual crop during the period beginning on , and ending on , shall be subject to a reduction in benefits under this section as described in this subparagraph.February 8, 2014December 20, 2018

children.label: aa
children.text: during the first 10 crop years after the initial tillage; and

children.label: bb
children.text: during which a crop on that acreage is enrolled under subsection ()(2) or (k).l

children.heading: De minimis acreage exemption
children.label: ii De minimis acreage exemption
children.text: The Secretary shall exempt areas of 5 acres or less from clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: the approved yield shall be determined by using a yield equal to 65 percent of the county expected yield; and

children.label: II
children.text: the service fees or premiums for crops planted on native sod shall be equal to 200 percent of the amount determined in subsections ()(2) or (k), as applicable, but in no case shall exceed the amount determined in subsection ()(2)(B)(ii).1So in original. Probably should be “subsection”.1ll

children.heading: Application
children.label: C Application
children.text: This paragraph shall only apply to native sod acreage in the States of Minnesota, Iowa, North Dakota, South Dakota, Montana, and Nebraska.

children.heading: Application for noninsured crop disaster assistance
children.label: b Application for noninsured crop disaster assistance

children.heading: Timely application
children.label: 1 Timely application
children.text: To be eligible for assistance under this section, a producer shall submit an application for noninsured crop disaster assistance at a local office of the Department. The application shall be in such form, contain such information, and be submitted by an appropriate deadline before the beginning of the coverage period, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Records
children.label: 2 Records
children.text: To be eligible for assistance under this section, a producer shall provide annually to the Secretary records of crop acreage, acreage yields, and production for each crop, as required by the Secretary.

children.heading: Acreage reports
children.label: 3 Acreage reports
children.text: A producer shall provide annual reports on acreage planted or prevented from being planted, as required by the Secretary, by the designated acreage reporting date for the crop and location as established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Streamlined submission process
children.label: 4 Streamlined submission process
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a streamlined process for the submission of records and acreage reports under paragraphs (2) and (3) for diverse production systems such as those typical of urban production systems, other small-scale production systems, and direct-to-consumer production systems.

children.heading: Cause
children.label: 1 Cause
children.text: To be eligible for assistance under this section, a producer of an eligible crop shall have suffered a loss of a noninsured commodity as the result of a cause described in subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On making a determination described in subsection (a)(3), the Secretary shall provide assistance under this section to producers of an eligible crop that have suffered a loss as a result of the cause described in subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: Aquaculture producers
children.label: B Aquaculture producers
children.text: On making a determination described in subsection (a)(3) for aquaculture producers, the Secretary shall provide assistance under this section to aquaculture producers from all losses related to drought.

children.heading: Prevented planting
children.label: 3 Prevented planting
children.text: Subject to paragraph (1), the Secretary shall make a prevented planting noninsured crop disaster assistance payment if the producer is prevented from planting more than 35 percent of the acreage intended for the eligible crop because of drought, flood, or other natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Area trigger
children.label: 4 Area trigger
children.text: The Secretary shall provide assistance to individual producers without any requirement of an area loss.
the producer’s share of the total acres devoted to the eligible crop; by
the quantity that is less than 50 percent of the approved yield for the crop, as determined by the Secretary; by

children.text: in the case of each of the 1996 through 1998 crop years, 60 percent of the average market price for the crop (or any comparable coverage determined by the Secretary); or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of each of the 1999 and subsequent crop years, 55 percent of the average market price for the crop (or any comparable coverage determined by the Secretary); by

children.text: harvested;

children.label: ii

children.label: iii
children.text: prevented from being planted because of drought, flood, or other natural disaster (as determined by the Secretary); and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a crop that is not produced with a significant and variable harvesting expense, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish approved yields for purposes of providing noninsured crop disaster assistance under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine an approved yield using the actual production history of the producer over a period of not less than the 4 previous consecutive crop years and not more than 10 consecutive crop years.

children.heading: Calculation
children.label: B Calculation
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), the approved yield for the year in which noninsured crop disaster assistance is sought shall be equal to the average of the actual production history of the producer during the period considered.

children.heading: Assignment of yield
children.label: 3 Assignment of yield
children.text: If a producer does not submit adequate documentation of production history to determine a crop yield under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall assign to the producer a yield equal to not less than 65 percent of the county expected yield (adjusted to reflect actual production reflected in the records acceptable to the Secretary for continuous years), as specified in regulations issued by the Secretary based on production history requirements.

children.heading: Prohibition on assigned yields in certain counties
children.label: 4 Prohibition on assigned yields in certain counties

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: i Documentation
children.text: If sufficient data are available to demonstrate that the acreage of a crop in a county for the crop year has increased by more than 100 percent over any year in the preceding 7 crop years or, if data are not available, if the acreage of the crop in the county has increased significantly from the previous crop years, a producer must provide such detailed documentation of production costs, acres planted, and yield for the crop year for which benefits are being claimed as is required by the Secretary. If the Secretary determines that the documentation provided is not sufficient, the Secretary may require documenting proof that the crop, had the crop been harvested, could have been marketed at a reasonable price.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: ii Prohibition
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), a producer who produces a crop on a farm located in a county described in clause (i) may not obtain an assigned yield.

children.label: i
children.text: the planted acreage of the producer for the crop has been inspected by a third party acceptable to the Secretary; or

children.label: I
children.text: the County Executive Director and the State Executive Director recommend an exemption from the requirement to the Administrator of the Agency; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Administrator approves the recommendation.

children.heading: Limitation on receipt of subsequent assigned yield
children.label: 5 Limitation on receipt of subsequent assigned yield
children.text: A producer who receives an assigned yield for the current year of a natural disaster because required production records were not submitted to the local office of the Department shall not be eligible for an assigned yield for the year of the next natural disaster unless the required production records of the previous 1 or more years (as applicable) are provided to the local office.

children.heading: Yield variations due to different farming practices
children.label: 6 Yield variations due to different farming practices
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that noninsured crop disaster assistance accurately reflects significant yield variations due to different farming practices, such as between irrigated and nonirrigated acreage.

children.heading: Contract payments
children.label: f Contract payments
children.text: A producer who has received a guaranteed payment for production, as opposed to delivery, of a crop pursuant to a contract shall have the production of the producer adjusted upward by the amount of the production equal to the amount of the contract payment received.

children.heading: Use of Commodity Credit Corporation
children.label: g Use of Commodity Credit Corporation
children.text: The Secretary may use the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation to carry out this section.
the neglect or malfeasance of the producer;
the failure of the producer to reseed to the same crop in those areas and under such circumstances where it is customary to reseed; or
the failure of the producer to follow good farming practices, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 1 Definitions
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “legal entity” and “person” have the meanings given those terms in .section 1308(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of catastrophic coverage under subsection (c), $125,000; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of additional coverage under subsection (), $300,000.l

children.heading: Limitation on multiple benefits for same loss
children.label: 3 Limitation on multiple benefits for same loss

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), if a producer who is eligible to receive benefits under this section is also eligible to receive assistance for the same loss under any other program administered by the Secretary, the producer shall be required to elect whether to receive benefits under this section or under the other program, but not both.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to emergency loans under subtitle C of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1961

children.heading: Adjusted gross income limitation
children.label: 4 Adjusted gross income limitation
children.text: A person or legal entity that has an average adjusted gross income in excess of the average adjusted gross income limitation applicable under section 1308–3a(b)(1)(A) of this title, or a successor provision, shall not be eligible to receive noninsured crop disaster assistance under this section.2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: A
children.text: to ensure a fair and equitable application of , the general payment limitation regulations of the Secretary, and the limitations established under this subsection; andsection 1308 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to ensure that payments under this section are attributed to a person or legal entity (excluding a joint venture or general partnership) in accordance with the terms and conditions of sections 1308 through 1308–3a of this title, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: $325 per crop per county; or

children.label: B
children.text: $825 per producer per county, but not to exceed a total of $1,950 per producer.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary shall waive the service fee required under paragraph (1) in the case of a limited resource, beginning, or socially disadvantaged farmer, as defined by the Secretary, or a veteran farmer or rancher (as defined in ).section 2279(a) of this title

children.heading: Use
children.label: 3 Use
children.text: The Secretary shall deposit service fees collected under this subsection in the Commodity Credit Corporation Fund.

children.label: l Payment equivalent to additional coverage

children.heading: Limited resource, beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged farmers
children.label: 3 Limited resource, beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged farmers
children.text: The coverage made available under this subsection shall be available to limited resource, beginning, and socially disadvantaged farmers, as determined by the Secretary, and veteran farmers or ranchers (as defined in ) in exchange for a premium that is 50 percent of the premium determined under paragraph (2).section 2279(a) of this title
